channel.title: PYT Radar
channel.description: Podcast & YouTube Tracking ГғВўГӮВҖГӮВ” automated episode digest. Tracking mentions of Karen Bass, Nithya Raman, and more across podcasts and YouTube.

item.title: LA Podcast: Hosts analyze K Line approval and conflicting mayoral polls showing Bass with historically soft support
item.description:
LA Podcast
"I Heard It Through The K Line" вҖ“ Published April 6, 2026

Match: Karen Bass (guest), Nithya Raman (topic)

Sentiment: Negative

Hosts Scott Frazier, Mike Bonin, and Godfrey Plata examine MetroвҖҷs approval of the K Line northern extension and analyze conflicting mayoral polls. The LMU poll shows Nithya Raman leading Bass nearly 2-to-1, while the UCLA Luskin poll has Bass at 25% with Raman at 9%. All three hosts agree BassвҖҷs support is historically soft for an incumbent.

Key moment at 39:18: вҖңMayor BassвҖҷs actual support might be something like 22 to 24%. ThatвҖҷs terrible.вҖқ вҖ“ Scott Frazier

в–¶ Listen on Apple Podcasts | в–¶ Listen on Spotify

item.title: LA Podcast: Hosts analyze K Line approval and conflicting mayoral polls showing Bass with historically soft support
item.description:
LA Podcast
"I Heard It Through The K Line" \u2013 Published April 6, 2026

Match: Karen Bass (guest), Nithya Raman (topic)

Sentiment: Negative

Hosts Scott Frazier, Mike Bonin, and Godfrey Plata examine Metro\u2019s approval of the K Line northern extension and analyze conflicting mayoral polls. The LMU poll shows Nithya Raman leading Bass nearly 2-to-1, while the UCLA Luskin poll has Bass at 25% with Raman at 9%. All three hosts agree Bass\u2019s support is historically soft for an incumbent.

Key moment at 39:18: "Mayor Bass\u2019s actual support might be something like 22 to 24%. That\u2019s terrible." \u2013 Scott Frazier

item.title: LA Podcast: Hosts analyze K Line approval and conflicting mayoral polls showing Bass with historically soft support
item.description:
LA Podcast
"I Heard It Through The K Line" \u2013 Published April 6, 2026

Match: Karen Bass (guest), Nithya Raman (topic)

Sentiment: Negative

Hosts Scott Frazier, Mike Bonin, and Godfrey Plata examine Metro\u2019s approval of the K Line northern extension and analyze conflicting mayoral polls. The LMU poll shows Nithya Raman leading Bass nearly 2-to-1, while the UCLA Luskin poll has Bass at 25% with Raman at 9%. All three hosts agree Bass\u2019s support is historically soft for an incumbent.

Key moment at 39:18: "Mayor Bass\u2019s actual support might be something like 22 to 24%. That\u2019s terrible." \u2013 Scott Frazier
